description: Enterokinase (enteropeptidase) recognition/cleavage site DDDDK — a 5-residue protease site used to remove an N-terminal fusion tag, leaving no extra residues on the downstream protein.

label: enterokinase_site
note: Enterokinase (enteropeptidase) recognition/cleavage site (Asp-Asp-Asp-Asp-Lys). The protease cleaves on the C-terminal side of the Lys (after K), releasing a downstream protein with no residual vector-derived residues — hence its wide use for tag removal from fusion proteins. The acidic (Asp)4 tetrad and the P1 Lys are recognized by the enterokinase light-chain S1–S5 basic substrate pocket. Sequence DDDDK is a 100%-identity match to residues 19–23 of the trypsinogen-1 activation propeptide (UniProt:P00760), the canonical natural occurrence of the motif. This part covers a 5-aa sub-span of that 247-aa precursor (full_length_match=false), which is expected for a short recognition motif and is not a sequence defect.

provenance.sequence_source: UniProt:P00760 (TRY1_BOVIN, Bos taurus trypsinogen-1, Swiss-Prot reviewed PE=1 SV=3) — DDDDK is an exact 100% match to residues 19–23 of the 247-aa precursor, within the activation propeptide (annotated positions 18–23). Verified by independent FASTA fetch and Python substring search.

functional_claims.label: DDDDK is the enterokinase (enteropeptidase) recognition sequence; the enzyme cleaves on the C-terminal side of the Lys, leaving no extra residues on the downstream protein

source.quote: Purification and specificity of porcine enterokinase

functional_claims.label: The canonical recognition motif is (Asp)4-Lys-Ile (DDDDK↓I); the four upstream Asp residues and a P1 Lys define the site
DDDDK is used as a fusion-protein tag-removal site; recombinant enterokinase light chain cleaves it to release the target protein

source.quote: Production of recombinant bovine enterokinase catalytic subunit in Escherichia coli — used to cleave DDDDK fusion sites

functional_claims.label: Enterokinase is not strictly specific: it can cleave at secondary/off-target sites and prefers Arg over Lys at P1; canonical DDDDK is not its fastest substrate

value.downstream_context: P1'=Ser followed by a basic P2' (e.g. SRLLR) enhances cleavage

source.quote: enteropeptidase is unexpectedly promiscuous; prefers Arg at P1 over Lys; canonical DDDDK is not the fastest substrate

functional_claims.label: The DDDDR variant (P1 Lys→Arg) is cleaved more efficiently, requiring 3–6x less enzyme than DDDDK for equivalent fusion-protein cleavage

source.quote: DDDDR variant requires 3–6x less enzyme than DDDDK for equivalent cleavage of Trx/TRAIL and Trx/FGF-2 fusions
